```{shinylive-python}
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Python Chunk: merge()", value="import pandas as pd\nName = ['Ben', 'Alice', 'Daniel', 'Charlie']\nYear = [1990, 1992, 1995, 1993]\nOption = ['Biology','Physics', 'Chemistry', 'Mathematics']\nStudents = pd.DataFrame({'First_Name': Name, 'Year': Year, 'Options': Option})\n\nFull_Names = pd.DataFrame({'First_Name': ['Alice','Ben','Daniel'],\n                           'Last_Name': ['Parker','James','Brown']})\nprint(pd.merge(Students, Full_Names))             # nur gemeinsame Einträge\nprint(pd.merge(Students, Full_Names, how='outer')) # alle Einträge", width='100%', height='210px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    def out():
        f = io.StringIO()
        namespace = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value), '<string>', 'eval'), namespace)
                        if val is not None:
                            print(val)
                    else:
                        exec(compile(ast.Module([node], []), '<string>', 'exec'), namespace)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Fehler: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

---

## Übungen & Quiz

### Quiz 1: Listen & Arrays

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: LISTEN & ARRAYS</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Probiere hier deinen Code aus:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 220
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – Listen & Arrays", value="import numpy as np\n# Hier kannst du alles ausprobieren!\n", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    def out():
        f = io.StringIO()
        namespace = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value), '<string>', 'eval'), namespace)
                        if val is not None:
                            print(val)
                    else:
                        exec(compile(ast.Module([node], []), '<string>', 'exec'), namespace)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Fehler: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Frage 1:</strong> Was gibt <code>len([9, 3, 7, 2])</code> zurück?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv1" id="pv1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv1a">2</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv1" id="pv1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv1b">4</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv1" id="pv1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv1c">9</label></div>
    <div id="pv1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 2:</strong> Python verwendet 0-basierte Indizes. Wie greifst du auf das <strong>dritte</strong> Element von <code>y = [9, 3, 7, 2]</code> zu?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv2" id="pv2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv2a"><code>y[3]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv2" id="pv2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv2b"><code>y[2]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv2" id="pv2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv2c"><code>y[1]</code></label></div>
    <div id="pv2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 3:</strong> Warum gibt <code>sum(np.array([True, False, True, True]))</code> den Wert <code>3</code> zurück?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv3" id="pv3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv3a">Weil das Array 3 Elemente hat</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv3" id="pv3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv3b">Weil Python True als 1 und False als 0 interpretiert – sum() zählt also die True-Werte</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv3" id="pv3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv3c">Weil sum() nur die ersten drei Elemente zählt</label></div>
    <div id="pv3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 4:</strong> Was gibt <code>y[y &lt; 5]</code> zurück, wenn <code>y = np.array([9, 3, 7, 2])</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv4" id="pv4a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv4a">[9, 7]</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv4" id="pv4b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv4b">[3, 2]</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pv4" id="pv4c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pv4c">[True, False, True, True]</label></div>
    <div id="pv4-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <button onclick="checkPyVektorQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100"
    style="background-color: #1a4d1a; border: none; font-weight: 700;">ANTWORTEN PRÜFEN</button>
  <div id="py-vektor-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>

<script>
function checkPyVektorQuiz() {
  var score = 0; var total = 4;

  var pv1 = document.querySelector('input[name="pv1"]:checked');
  var pv1f = document.getElementById('pv1-feedback');
  if (pv1 && pv1.id === 'pv1b') {
    pv1f.innerHTML = '✅ Richtig! Die Liste hat 4 Elemente: 9, 3, 7, 2.'; pv1f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pv1) {
    pv1f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht ganz. Zähle die Elemente: 9, 3, 7, 2 – das sind 4.'; pv1f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pv2 = document.querySelector('input[name="pv2"]:checked');
  var pv2f = document.getElementById('pv2-feedback');
  if (pv2 && pv2.id === 'pv2b') {
    pv2f.innerHTML = '✅ Genau! In Python beginnt die Zählung bei 0. Das dritte Element hat also Index 2: y[0]=9, y[1]=3, y[2]=7.'; pv2f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pv2) {
    pv2f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht korrekt. Python ist 0-basiert: y[0]=9 (1. Element), y[1]=3 (2. Element), y[2]=7 (3. Element).'; pv2f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pv3 = document.querySelector('input[name="pv3"]:checked');
  var pv3f = document.getElementById('pv3-feedback');
  if (pv3 && pv3.id === 'pv3b') {
    pv3f.innerHTML = '✅ Genau! Auch in Python gilt True = 1 und False = 0. sum([1,0,1,1]) = 3.'; pv3f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pv3) {
    pv3f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht richtig. True = 1 und False = 0 – sum() addiert also die Einsen und zählt damit die True-Werte.'; pv3f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pv4 = document.querySelector('input[name="pv4"]:checked');
  var pv4f = document.getElementById('pv4-feedback');
  if (pv4 && pv4.id === 'pv4b') {
    pv4f.innerHTML = '✅ Richtig! y < 5 testet jedes Element: 9<5=False, 3<5=True, 7<5=False, 2<5=True. Also werden 3 und 2 zurückgegeben.'; pv4f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pv4) {
    pv4f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht ganz. Probiere es in der Sandbox! y[y < 5] gibt nur die Elemente zurück, für die die Bedingung True ist.'; pv4f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var result = document.getElementById('py-vektor-result');
  if (score === total) {
    result.innerHTML = '🎉 Alle Fragen richtig!'; result.style.color = '#1a4d1a';
    if (typeof markTaskDone === 'function') markTaskDone('woche2_py_listen');
  } else {
    result.innerHTML = score + ' von ' + total + ' richtig. Schau dir die Erklärungen an!'; result.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }
}
</script>
```

---

### Quiz 2: Matrizen

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: MATRIZEN</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Probiere hier deinen Code aus:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 220
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – Matrizen", value="import numpy as np\nX = np.arange(1, 7).reshape(2, 3)\nprint(X)\nprint(X.shape)", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    def out():
        f = io.StringIO()
        namespace = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value), '<string>', 'eval'), namespace)
                        if val is not None:
                            print(val)
                    else:
                        exec(compile(ast.Module([node], []), '<string>', 'exec'), namespace)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Fehler: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Frage 1:</strong> Was gibt <code>X.shape</code> für <code>np.arange(1,7).reshape(2,3)</code> zurück?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm1" id="pm1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm1a">6</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm1" id="pm1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm1b">(2, 3)</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm1" id="pm1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm1c">(3, 2)</label></div>
    <div id="pm1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 2:</strong> Was ist der Unterschied zwischen <code>np.column_stack()</code> und <code>np.row_stack()</code>?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm2" id="pm2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm2a">Kein Unterschied</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm2" id="pm2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm2b"><code>column_stack()</code> fügt Vektoren als Spalten zusammen, <code>row_stack()</code> als Zeilen</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm2" id="pm2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm2c"><code>column_stack()</code> funktioniert nur für Zahlen</label></div>
    <div id="pm2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 3:</strong> Was gibt <code>X[1, :]</code> zurück?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm3" id="pm3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm3a">Das Element in Zeile 1, Spalte 0</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm3" id="pm3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm3b">Alle Elemente der zweiten Zeile (Index 1)</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pm3" id="pm3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pm3c">Alle Elemente der zweiten Spalte</label></div>
    <div id="pm3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <button onclick="checkPyMatrixQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100"
    style="background-color: #1a4d1a; border: none; font-weight: 700;">ANTWORTEN PRÜFEN</button>
  <div id="py-matrix-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>

<script>
function checkPyMatrixQuiz() {
  var score = 0; var total = 3;

  var pm1 = document.querySelector('input[name="pm1"]:checked');
  var pm1f = document.getElementById('pm1-feedback');
  if (pm1 && pm1.id === 'pm1b') {
    pm1f.innerHTML = '✅ Richtig! .shape gibt (Zeilen, Spalten) zurück – also (2, 3).'; pm1f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pm1) {
    pm1f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht ganz. .shape gibt immer (Zeilen, Spalten) zurück. Bei reshape(2,3) ist das (2, 3).'; pm1f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pm2 = document.querySelector('input[name="pm2"]:checked');
  var pm2f = document.getElementById('pm2-feedback');
  if (pm2 && pm2.id === 'pm2b') {
    pm2f.innerHTML = '✅ Genau! column_stack fügt Vektoren als Spalten an, row_stack als Zeilen. Probiere row_stack in der Sandbox!'; pm2f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pm2) {
    pm2f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht richtig. column_stack = Spalten zusammenfügen, row_stack = Zeilen zusammenfügen.'; pm2f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pm3 = document.querySelector('input[name="pm3"]:checked');
  var pm3f = document.getElementById('pm3-feedback');
  if (pm3 && pm3.id === 'pm3b') {
    pm3f.innerHTML = '✅ Korrekt! X[1, :] bedeutet: Zeile mit Index 1 (= 2. Zeile), alle Spalten (:).'; pm3f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pm3) {
    pm3f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht ganz. Die Syntax ist X[Zeile, Spalte]. Der Doppelpunkt : bedeutet "alles". X[1, :] = alle Spalten der zweiten Zeile (Index 1).'; pm3f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var result = document.getElementById('py-matrix-result');
  if (score === total) {
    result.innerHTML = '🎉 Perfekt!'; result.style.color = '#1a4d1a';
    if (typeof markTaskDone === 'function') markTaskDone('woche2_py_matrizen');
  } else {
    result.innerHTML = score + ' von ' + total + ' richtig.'; result.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }
}
</script>
```

---

### Quiz 3: Dictionaries

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: DICTIONARIES</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Probiere hier deinen Code aus:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 220
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – Dictionaries", value="dict1 = {'color': ['blue', 'red'], 'size': 5, 'state': True}\n# Was ist der Typ?\nprint(type(dict1['color']))\nprint(dict1['color'][1])", width='100%', height='100px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    def out():
        f = io.StringIO()
        namespace = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value), '<string>', 'eval'), namespace)
                        if val is not None:
                            print(val)
                    else:
                        exec(compile(ast.Module([node], []), '<string>', 'exec'), namespace)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Fehler: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Frage 1:</strong> Wie greifst du auf den Wert des Keys <code>'color'</code> in <code>dict1 = {'color': ['blue', 'red'], 'size': 5}</code> zu?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl1" id="pl1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl1a"><code>dict1[0]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl1" id="pl1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl1b"><code>dict1['color']</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl1" id="pl1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl1c"><code>dict1.color</code></label></div>
    <div id="pl1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 2:</strong> Wie greifst du auf die <strong>zweite Position</strong> des Werts von <code>'color'</code> zu?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl2" id="pl2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl2a"><code>dict1['color'][2]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl2" id="pl2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl2b"><code>dict1['color'][1]</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl2" id="pl2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl2c"><code>dict1[1]['color']</code></label></div>
    <div id="pl2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 3:</strong> Wie löschst du den Key <code>'size'</code> aus einem Dictionary?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl3" id="pl3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl3a"><code>dict1['size'] = None</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl3" id="pl3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl3b"><code>del dict1['size']</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pl3" id="pl3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pl3c"><code>dict1.remove('size')</code></label></div>
    <div id="pl3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <button onclick="checkPyDictQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100"
    style="background-color: #1a4d1a; border: none; font-weight: 700;">ANTWORTEN PRÜFEN</button>
  <div id="py-dict-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>

<script>
function checkPyDictQuiz() {
  var score = 0; var total = 3;

  var pl1 = document.querySelector('input[name="pl1"]:checked');
  var pl1f = document.getElementById('pl1-feedback');
  if (pl1 && pl1.id === 'pl1b') {
    pl1f.innerHTML = '✅ Richtig! Bei Dictionaries greifst du mit dem Key-Namen zu: dict1["color"].'; pl1f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pl1) {
    pl1f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht ganz. Dictionaries werden über ihren Key angesprochen, nicht über einen Index. dict1["color"] ist korrekt.'; pl1f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pl2 = document.querySelector('input[name="pl2"]:checked');
  var pl2f = document.getElementById('pl2-feedback');
  if (pl2 && pl2.id === 'pl2b') {
    pl2f.innerHTML = '✅ Korrekt! dict1["color"] gibt die Liste ["blue", "red"] zurück. Das zweite Element hat Index 1 (0-basiert): dict1["color"][1] = "red".'; pl2f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pl2) {
    pl2f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht korrekt. Zuerst den Wert holen: dict1["color"] → ["blue", "red"]. Dann das zweite Element (Index 1): dict1["color"][1].'; pl2f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pl3 = document.querySelector('input[name="pl3"]:checked');
  var pl3f = document.getElementById('pl3-feedback');
  if (pl3 && pl3.id === 'pl3b') {
    pl3f.innerHTML = '✅ Genau! del dict1["size"] entfernt den Key samt seinem Wert vollständig aus dem Dictionary.'; pl3f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pl3) {
    pl3f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht richtig. dict1["size"] = None lässt den Key bestehen, setzt aber den Wert auf None. del dict1["size"] entfernt ihn komplett.'; pl3f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var result = document.getElementById('py-dict-result');
  if (score === total) {
    result.innerHTML = '🎉 Alle richtig!'; result.style.color = '#1a4d1a';
    if (typeof markTaskDone === 'function') markTaskDone('woche2_py_dicts');
  } else {
    result.innerHTML = score + ' von ' + total + ' richtig.'; result.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }
}
</script>
```

---

### Quiz 4: DataFrames

```{=html}
<div class="quiz-card">
  <h5>QUIZ: DATAFRAMES</h5>
  <p class="text-muted">🧪 Probiere hier deinen Code aus:</p>
```

```{shinylive-python}
#| standalone: true
#| viewerHeight: 250
from shiny import App, ui, render, reactive
import io, contextlib, ast
import numpy as np

app_ui = ui.page_fluid(
    ui.input_text_area("code", "Sandbox – DataFrames", value="import pandas as pd\nProduct_name = ['orange', 'strawberry', 'broccoli', 'blueberry', 'cucumber']\nFruit = [True, True, False, True, False]\nColor = ['orange', 'red', 'green', 'blue', 'green']\ndf = pd.DataFrame({'Product_name': Product_name, 'Fruit': Fruit, 'Color': Color})\nprint(df)", width='100%', height='140px'),
    ui.input_action_button("run", "Run Code", class_="btn-success"),
    ui.output_text_verbatim("out")
)
def server(input, output, session):
    @render.text
    @reactive.event(input.run)
    def out():
        f = io.StringIO()
        namespace = {}
        with contextlib.redirect_stdout(f):
            try:
                tree = ast.parse(input.code())
                for node in tree.body:
                    if isinstance(node, ast.Expr):
                        val = eval(compile(ast.Expression(node.value), '<string>', 'eval'), namespace)
                        if val is not None:
                            print(val)
                    else:
                        exec(compile(ast.Module([node], []), '<string>', 'exec'), namespace)
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Fehler: {e}")
        return f.getvalue()
app = App(app_ui, server)
```

```{=html}
  <div class="mb-4 mt-3">
    <p><strong>Frage 1:</strong> Was ist der wichtigste Unterschied zwischen einem pandas DataFrame und einer NumPy-Matrix?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd1" id="pd1a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd1a">DataFrames können größer sein</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd1" id="pd1b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd1b">DataFrames können verschiedene Datentypen in verschiedenen Spalten enthalten, NumPy-Arrays nur einen Typ</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd1" id="pd1c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd1c">NumPy-Arrays haben immer Spaltennamen</label></div>
    <div id="pd1-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 2:</strong> Wie viele Früchte enthält <code>Fruit = [True, True, False, True, False]</code>? Welche Funktion nutzt du?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd2" id="pd2a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd2a">2, mit <code>len(Fruit)</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd2" id="pd2b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd2b">3, mit <code>sum(Fruit)</code></label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd2" id="pd2c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd2c">5, mit <code>sum(Fruit)</code></label></div>
    <div id="pd2-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <div class="mb-4">
    <p><strong>Frage 3:</strong> Was macht <code>pd.merge(Students, Full_Names)</code> standardmäßig?</p>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd3" id="pd3a"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd3a">Alle Zeilen beider DataFrames werden zusammengeführt, fehlende mit NaN gefüllt</label></div>
    <div class="form-check"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd3" id="pd3b"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd3b">Nur Zeilen, die in beiden DataFrames vorkommen, werden zusammengeführt (Inner Join)</label></div>
    <div class="form-check mb-2"><input class="form-check-input" type="radio" name="pd3" id="pd3c"><label class="form-check-label" for="pd3c">Die DataFrames werden untereinander (Zeilen) zusammengefügt</label></div>
    <div id="pd3-feedback" class="mt-1 fw-bold"></div>
  </div>

  <button onclick="checkPyDfQuiz()" class="btn btn-primary w-100"
    style="background-color: #1a4d1a; border: none; font-weight: 700;">ANTWORTEN PRÜFEN</button>
  <div id="py-df-result" class="mt-3 fw-bold text-center"></div>
</div>

<script>
function checkPyDfQuiz() {
  var score = 0; var total = 3;

  var pd1 = document.querySelector('input[name="pd1"]:checked');
  var pd1f = document.getElementById('pd1-feedback');
  if (pd1 && pd1.id === 'pd1b') {
    pd1f.innerHTML = '✅ Richtig! Ein DataFrame kann z.B. eine Spalte mit Zahlen und eine mit Text haben – ein NumPy-Array würde alles in denselben Typ konvertieren.'; pd1f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pd1) {
    pd1f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht ganz. Der Schlüsselunterschied liegt in den Datentypen: NumPy-Arrays enthalten nur einen Typ, DataFrames können verschiedene Typen spaltenweise mischen.'; pd1f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pd2 = document.querySelector('input[name="pd2"]:checked');
  var pd2f = document.getElementById('pd2-feedback');
  if (pd2 && pd2.id === 'pd2b') {
    pd2f.innerHTML = '✅ Korrekt! sum() auf einer Boolean-Liste zählt die True-Werte (True=1). Es gibt 3 Früchte: orange, strawberry und blueberry.'; pd2f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pd2) {
    pd2f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht richtig. len() gibt die Gesamtlänge zurück (5). sum() zählt die True-Werte – also die Früchte. Probiere es in der Sandbox!'; pd2f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var pd3 = document.querySelector('input[name="pd3"]:checked');
  var pd3f = document.getElementById('pd3-feedback');
  if (pd3 && pd3.id === 'pd3b') {
    pd3f.innerHTML = '✅ Genau! Standardmäßig ist how="inner" – nur Zeilen mit übereinstimmenden Keys in beiden DataFrames erscheinen. Mit how="outer" werden alle Zeilen behalten und fehlende mit NaN gefüllt.'; pd3f.style.color = '#1a4d1a'; score++;
  } else if (pd3) {
    pd3f.innerHTML = '❌ Nicht korrekt. pd.merge() verbindet DataFrames über gemeinsame Spalten. Ohne how="outer" werden nur Zeilen behalten, die in beiden vorkommen.'; pd3f.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }

  var result = document.getElementById('py-df-result');
  if (score === total) {
    result.innerHTML = '🎉 Ausgezeichnet – Woche 2 Python abgeschlossen!'; result.style.color = '#1a4d1a';
    if (typeof markTaskDone === 'function') markTaskDone('woche2_py_dataframes');
  } else {
    result.innerHTML = score + ' von ' + total + ' richtig.'; result.style.color = '#d9534f';
  }
}
</script>
```

:::
